title: Text Inspection, Search, and Structured Transformation · Linux Operations Room
summary: Extract a defensible incident summary from mixed logs without silently corrupting delimiters, encodings, ordering, or duplicate policy. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal.
Use fixed strings, basic or extended regular expressions, anchors, classes, context, and exit status with explicit locale assumptions.
Separate find traversal, predicates, grouping, pruning, printing, and actions while protecting path boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Logs contain duplicate IDs, spaces, UTF-8 names, and severities high, low, and HIGH. Produce a stable 4-row summary with explicit locale, delimiter, regex, sort key, and duplicate policy.

What must be established before implementation?
